steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Views increase by 40% every 3 days, starting at 245. A 40% increase means multiplying by 1.40 (not 0.40). The exponent must count how many 3-day periods fit in x days, which is x/3 (not 3x).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $f(x)=245(0.40)^{\frac{x}{3}}$: Base 0.40 means a 60% decrease each period — the opposite of growth. ✗
  • Choice B — $f(x)=245(0.40)^{3x}$: Wrong base (decay) and wrong exponent (3x triples the periods). ✗
  • Choice C — $f(x)=245(1.40)^{\frac{x}{3}}$: Base 1.40 gives a 40% increase per period; exponent x/3 counts 3-day intervals correctly. ✓
  • Choice D — $f(x)=245(1.40)^{3x}$: Correct base but exponent 3x makes the period ⅓ a day instead of 3 days — grows far too fast. ✗
C is correct.
